weak_spots: inter-step dependency when splitting prompts — needs to watch for lost context across split prompts
1.evidence: Correctly applied the rule to the transfer scenario and backed it with concrete, specific, personal practice details — including real friction (lost context, ordering surprise, a cross-reference fix).

weak_spots: role assignment — understood conceptually but not yet applied in own prompts
inter-step dependency when splitting prompts — needs to watch for lost context across split prompts
3.evidence: Student gave correct conceptual and transfer answers but admitted no practice — first offered hypotheticals, then conceded directly. No concrete personal example or friction to back up the knowledge.

1.attempted: used 1-task-1-verb on a meal-prep prompt that asked an assistant to 'plan meals for the week and also make a grocery list and estimate the cost'
1.concrete_detail: turned it into three numbered single-verb steps; the cost estimate only came out right once it was step 3 depending on step 2's list
1.snippet: the model kept giving me meals but silently skipping the cost line until I split it out
1.friction: I first tried two separate prompts instead of one numbered list and lost the shared context between the meals and the list
1.surprise: ordering mattered more than I expected — cost had to come after the grocery list or it just guessed a round number
1.outcome: one numbered list with cost last gave a grocery list that actually added up
